steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to contrast the two samples — NiTi wire and EPDM rubber. A contrast requires highlighting a difference between them. The notes provide two clear differences: thickness (0.7 mm vs. 2 mm) and temperature increase (21.3°C vs. 12°C). Any answer that merely mentions both samples without stating a difference, or that ignores both samples, fails the task.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In 2019, researchers studied the effect of torsional heating on various fibers, including samples of EPDM rubber and single-ply NiTi wire.: Mentions both samples but only groups them together as things studied — no difference between them is stated. This is a description of the study, not a contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — The EPDM rubber sample used in the 2019 study was thicker than the single ply NiTi wire sample.: States a direct difference between the two samples (EPDM 2 mm > NiTi 0.7 mm). This is a genuine contrast drawn from the notes. ✓
  • Choice C — Twisting an object will generally cause its temperature to increase, a process known as torsional heating.: Defines torsional heating in general — neither sample is mentioned. Fails to contrast anything. ✗
  • Choice D — When the fibers were twisted as part of the 2019 study, the surface temperature of both samples increased.: Notes that both temperatures rose — a similarity, not a contrast. Selecting this confuses contrast with comparison. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
